guardrails.title: Improve Naming
guardrails.body: For maintainability, improve variable, function, and class names when they do not accurately express their intent.

guardrails.title: Extract Duplicated Logic
guardrails.body: For maintainability, extract logic repeated in two or more places into a common helper.

guardrails.title: Split Large Functions
guardrails.body: For readability, split functions with multiple responsibilities into single-responsibility functions.

guardrails.title: Add Type Definitions
guardrails.body: For code quality, add type definitions to untyped code and strengthen weak types (any, unknown) when changing files.

guardrails.title: No Magic Values
guardrails.body: Do not use literal values (numbers, strings) whose meaning is not self-evident. Use named constants to clarify intent.

guardrails.title: Minimize Code Volume (DRY)
guardrails.body: Do not duplicate the same knowledge or logic in multiple places (DRY principle). Minimize code volume and prefer deriving from a single source.
